content.body: Noam Rosenthal: Which APIs would accept TrustedParserOptions?
content.formatted_body: Noam Rosenthal: Which APIs would accept TrustedParserOptions?

content.body: whatever accepts a SetHTMLUnsafeOptions
Eg setHTMLUnsafe
And beforeHTMLUnsafe, streamHTMLUnsafe etc
Okay that makes sense.
I didn't see this, but I guess those changes go into the HTML spec.
Otherwise we could have just eliminated that type completely.
What is the ineraction if you pass both TrustedHTML and TrustedParserOptions to setHTMLUnsafe?
There is a draft PR (https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/12583). Waiting for https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/12528 to land first.
Something like `trusted_options.sanitizer.sanitize(trusted_html)`
What type?

you mean `TrustedHTML`? Yea I think it would be great to completely deprecate that and use enforced sanitizer instead. not sure re. web-compat etc and if people use TT for weird stuff like escaping instead of sanitizing
content.formatted_body: you mean TrustedHTML? Yea I think it would be great to completely deprecate that and use enforced sanitizer instead. not sure re. web-compat etc and if people use TT for weird stuff like escaping instead of sanitizing

content.body: FYI I will be OOO June 29 – July 31

Hi, when the HTML spec talks about "obtaining images on demand" (e.g. https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/images.html#:~:text=When%20obtaining%20images%20on%20demand), this refers to a browser not fetching images by default and only doing so when the user requests it, right?
Yes, it says so two paragraphs prior.
oh, right 👊
* oh, right 🤦
jmdyck: if you want me to use NewGlobalEnvironment just say it, it's pretty easy to do, just not sure if it's better or not. But you might be able to judge that better.
content.formatted_body: jmdyck: if you want me to use NewGlobalEnvironment just say it, it's pretty easy to do, just not sure if it's better or not. But you might be able to judge that better.

content.body: No, not `TrustedHTML`. I first thought we might only need TrustedParserOptions internally, but it makes sense that setHTMLUnsafe accepts it now.
content.formatted_body: No, not TrustedHTML. I first thought we might only need TrustedParserOptions internally, but it makes sense that setHTMLUnsafe accepts it now.

content.body: I doubt we can remove `TrustedHTML` again.
content.formatted_body: I doubt we can remove TrustedHTML again.

content.body: I think it's slightly worse to import NewGlobalEnv. Not really up me, but nobody else was suggesting it.
ok gotcha!
jmdyck: okay, then I guess we need review for all the changes and can move ahead assuming there's no new issues.
content.formatted_body: jmdyck: okay, then I guess we need review for all the changes and can move ahead assuming there's no new issues.

content.body: Separately, I was going to bring up null vs undefined as the signal for "use the default", but then you changed null to undefined.
A reminder, please remove agenda+ from the issues and PRs which have been discussed already and don't need to be on the agenda anymore.
jmdyck: ah, I'm okay either way. Web platform typically uses null (if there's no need to distinguish), but undefined works.
content.formatted_body: jmdyck: ah, I'm okay either way. Web platform typically uses null (if there's no need to distinguish), but undefined works.

content.body: 'undefined' was the old signal for "use the default", before https://github.com/tc39/ecma262/pull/3139, which is maybe why I used it in https://github.com/tc39/ecma262/pull/3728
but now I'm thinking null would be better
So `html-dfn.js` should now mostly work for `whatpr.org`. Let me know if you run into any issues although I hope not as I already used enough of Tobie's time.
content.formatted_body: So html-dfn.js should now mostly work for whatpr.org. Let me know if you run into any issues although I hope not as I already used enough of Tobie's time.
